activities.name: Lollipop and jellyfish for the New Year with Lauren

activities.name: Taking it easy

activities.description: Good to have Herve back after a month of travel and illness

activities.name: Short ladder to finish off the week

activities.description: After a couple of miles that didn’t record

activities.name: Go north, old man!
activities.description: Wind and rain at my back on the way up… not so pleasant on my return

activities.name: Part 1 of “2 hills and 2 bakeries”
activities.description: With Lauren and Tamara, who lead busy lives and couldn’t stick around for part 2

activities.name: Part 2 of “2 hills and 2 bakeries”

activities.name: Let it snow
activities.description: Flurries were briefly falling

activities.name: Helping Alan on his Seattle Beach Census
activities.description: Jogging from water access point to water access point along the north and West Side of Lake Union, after which Alan returned to Cap Hill, and I took the straight route back to my starting point. Yes, it was cold.

activities.name: It’s not *that* cold
activities.description: Great run with Ana, Herve, Mintu, Jennifer, and Kenzo!

activities.name: Along the Green River
activities.description: With Herve and Mintu. Very flat and very sunny!

activities.name: Trying to think while running: Listening to Lawrence Krauss interview Scott Aaronson
activities.description: There’s nobody better to explain the very constrained opportunities for quantum computing than Aaronson, who is one of the godfathers of the field. I wish that Krauss hadn’t interrupted him so much. If interested, skip to about the middle of this loooong discussion: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-origins-podcast-with-lawrence-krauss/id1467481703?i=1000638739963

activities.name: Wet with icy patches

activities.name: Standard Tangle… at tempo+ pace

activities.name: Toasty tour of Tiger with the frisky Frenchman
activities.description: One lap of this loop was quite enough for me, but as I was driving away, heroic Herve was setting out on a second lap. Mon dieu!

activities.name: Another recovery run.
activities.description: My quads didn’t like all the descent on Monday

activities.name: Classic skiing with Chris after…
activities.description: Adopting a dog! Female, 2 year old, 10lb of cuteness. We haven’t named her yet. Taking suggestions!

activities.name: Off trail in the saltbush
activities.description: Not enough snow to make the going easy. Nice old slog.

activities.name: Short ski after…
activities.description: Picking up our new pooch from the shelter here in Hailey, Idaho. Considering: Cokie, Cookie, Coco, Charlie, or….?

activities.name: Bloody Glorious. Great walk with Andrew, then a swim on my way home.

activities.name: Sydney’s Inner West: Land of runners!
activities.description: Amazing trails for bikes and runners, and thousands of people out there on a Saturday morning, even though the humidity was at Singapore levels. I did well to make it as far as I did. Temp 80, humidity 87

activities.name: Along the Wingecarribee with Marg and Tony
Furber Steps walk
activities.description: Tested negative for COVID this morning, so thought I’d go for a walk. Overdid it a little, but all’s well that ends well.

activities.name: First run after Covid… starting gently
activities.description: Tested positive until this morning. Very glad to be mostly done with it.

activities.name: Bean Walking

activities.description: Felt pretty good!

activities.name: Lungs not quite back to normal

activities.name: She pulls like a sled dog…
activities.description:
…. Until she smells something of interest 3” in front of your foot! The excitement of a Bean run.

Photo from the aftermath of our visit to the emergency vet on Tuesday night after she ate something toxic on a walk. Maybe marijuana.

activities.name: Seward Park and surrounds with a big crew

activities.name: Standard Tangle #54
activities.description: Pretty good, though I did have one stop

activities.name: Easy is supposed to be easy
activities.description: But sometimes it isn’t. At least I kept my heart rate well down

activities.description: The usual sniff-n-pull.

activities.name: Crisp morning through and around the U
Walk back from rental car drop off with Chris
Perfect morning; pretty good jog
activities.description: I always feel sluggish after not running for a bunch of days, but by the end I was moving ok

activities.name: Runner Bean

activities.name: Slumped slice of toast
activities.description: Bit of a struggle. Not sure why

activities.name: Stopping and starting and weaving and darting with Bean
activities.description: The exciting thing is that you never know where your foot is going to be able to land. Very zen, until you break your ankle.

activities.name: An even dozen in the Arboretum on a beautiful spring morning
Here’s to Herve, Tim, and Rimma
activities.description: Who are running Boston right now. Go, go, go!

activities.name: Long and slow

activities.name: Group run with Caitlin and Wendy
activities.description: Beautiful morning; great group; steep hill!

activities.name: Run with group, then hike with Mattias and Hans
Thumbs up with Bean
activities.description: After most runs I do some push ups, and Bean assists by getting the sweat off my face. Check out the tongue in the second photo

activities.name: 4.17 miles each hour
activities.description: This is the exact route for my Backyard Ultra on June 1st. There are about 10 more slots available. f you would like more info, direct message me.

activities.name: Tangletown calibration run #55
activities.description: No complaints. Enjoyed the misty rain.

activities.name: Coffee with Herve
activities.description: Mid run coffee breaks are the best.

activities.name: The weird shaped runs are always Bean runs
activities.description: After the first mile she calmed down and ran mostly at my side. Shocking!

activities.name: Soos Creek with Vlad the Baclava man!
activities.description: And Herve, Anita, Mintu and Tom

activities.name: On mornings like this, it’s called Bean Lake

activities.description: So slow feels so good!

activities.name: Short recovery run
activities.description: Felt pretty good

activities.name: Car chase from Bellevue ends in our rock wall
activities.description: Armed robbery suspect, apprehended right in front of our garage. Exciting morning. Then Bean and I went for a jog around the lake

activities.name: Fun tour de Bridle Trails with Expect Delays and friends
activities.description: Probably about 1/2 a mile short due to not starting the watch

activities.name: Windy afternoon in the disco with Chris and Bean
Fast shoes or generous GPS?
activities.description: Not sure how, but I went about a minute faster on this course than recent usual

activities.name: Long and flat with Herve
activities.description: Not as easy as the last long run we did, but glad to get it done.

activities.name: Sweet run with Grace
activities.description: Great trails and smooth asphalt with Grace, then soaking rain and dodgy trails on my return. Very fun.

activities.name: Rock Creek trail loop to Maryland with Grace
Beara Peninsula walk
activities.description: First day on the trails with our Stanford travel/study group. Bus ride to the trail, RIB boat back to Kenmare where we are staying. Glorious wild country.

activities.name: Kerry Way towards Killarney
activities.description: Glaciated uplands, peat bogs, and Oak forests. Wonderful walk except for the midges at lunch.

activities.name: Walk / Boat / Walk in the Killarney National Park
Looking for trails and not finding them
activities.description: With Martha, who taught me about Linden trees. Thank you!

activities.name: Walk around the end of the Dingle Peninsula
activities.description: To a picnic lunch above a beach. Preceded by a sheep dog demo. Very impressive!

activities.name: Woods of Ross Castle with Martha
activities.description: Beautiful sunny morning. Took a taxi there, and attempted to get one back, but no go. So… Shanks’ pony!

activities.name: Cliffs of Moher on a rare bluebird day
On the Karst limestone of the Burren
Up Corkscrew hill in the fog with Martha
Exploring the stone island of Inisheer
activities.description: Closest of the Aran Islands. The stone walls were built densely to clear the fields of stone ahead of making their own soil with tons of seaweed per acre. Tough life!

activities.name: Intervals x4
activities.description: Martha’s idea. With a warm-up and warm-down

activities.name: Final Walk of our Ireland trip: Black Head traverse
activities.description: Big sky and brooding clouds over beautiful Galway Bay

activities.name: Chris runs again!
activities.description: Before we get on a flight back to DC

activities.name: Leg stretcher in Rock Creek Park
activities.description: Hadn’t seen the zoo loop part of the trail before. Very beautiful. Hot (for me) and humid, so a few stops and high heart rate

activities.name: Delicate flower wilts in the humidity
activities.description: But it was still a beautiful run. And check out the last photo taken from a bridge across Rock Creek.

activities.name: Amazing Chicago
activities.description: On a crisp spring morning. What a delight.

activities.name: Tangling with Bean
activities.description: It’s good to be home.

activities.name: T minus 2 days
activities.description:
This is the course runners will use on Saturday. *Almost* identical to last year, and quite different from year one.

If you aren’t participating please stop by during the day at the start/finish and say hello. I will be there from 9am until after dark (at least)

activities.name: Part of lap 3

activities.name: New trails at the disco

activities.name: Bean’s first hike
activities.description: And first accidental dip in the Snoqualmie River. She’s a bold one.

activities.description: Getting over a chest cold. Lots of coughing

activities.name: 1 lap in each direction. Small negative split
activities.description: Clockwise on the 2nd lap was about 1 minute faster than anti clockwise. Need to try the opposite order next time

activities.name: Twin Falls Wedge: 2 miles up, then 9 miles down
activities.description: Herve, the good sport, agreed to run this with me. Very fun, especially the unmarked side trail that took us from the SVT to Edgewick Rd near the end.

activities.name: It’s cooler in Carkeek
activities.description: At least in the morning. Great run with Expect Delays

activities.name: Return from Tuck and Robin Lakes

activities.description: My back had been acting up, but is feeling a little better today. Good enough for a jog

activities.name: Actually cool if you actually get up early

activities.name: Sparkling clear waters of Deception creek

activities.name: Good to be running a little
Bean slips her harness and takes off
activities.description: She was frolicking with a St. Bernard puppy who chased her for several blocks, crossing multiple streets before they stopped. Luckily, everybody survived. (Photo from the weekend in Leavenworth)

activities.name: Quarter miles at 7:20 per
activities.description: It’s a start!

activities.name: Long run for 6” legs
activities.description: I wonder if they make step counters for dogs?

activities.name: Running Bean makes for a good warm up

activities.name: To swim in Snow Lake

activities.name: Mid run coffee with the Frenchman
activities.description: Good to catch up with him before he runs a marathon on the Paris course the day after the big race.

activities.name: 12 minute VO2 max test
activities.description: The so called “Cooper Test” has you run as far as you can in 12 mins… suggests my VO2max is about 42 at the moment, which is pretty good for someone my age apparently

activities.name: Reverse Dayton with Lauren
activities.description: Lots of walking on a hot (for me) morning.

activities.name: Altitude and Air Quality

activities.name: Pythagoras of Ballard
activities.description: With Tamara, Jennifer, and Martha. Very sunny on the return.

activities.name: Pace ladder on Kenwood hill repeats
activities.description: Better than I expected. Nice and cool!

activities.name: Bean runs: Always slow; never easy

activities.name: The morning after Weston’s wedding
activities.description: Mid 60s by 7:30am sent me seeking shade in the neighborhood.

activities.name: Lake Union with Tamara and Herve
Success!
activities.description: Our 2nd annual Snake2lake is coming up in a few weeks and I wanted to see whether the 1/4 mile of extreme bushwhacking after Deep Creek could be avoided. The answer is yes! We found the “official” path which was actually easier than the blackberry choked descent to Raging River. Herve cut back a lot of blackberries on that section so it should be manageable for the event. Great morning,

activities.name: Feeling a bit off this morning
activities.description: Abandoned T & H to stick to the cool canyon.

activities.name: Intervals I guess
activities.description: Warm, humid, and a bit smoky it seems. Couldn’t go more than half a mile without a walk

activities.name: Expect Delays at Cougar Mountain
activities.description: Took a tumble and scraped my hand,  but Dr Uppal patched me up and I was able to run on. Thank you Anita!

activities.name: Twin Falls stroll with Margaret and Chris and Bean
Cascade lake camping with Roger and Nancy
Mountain Lake with Chris, Roger, Nancy and Bean
Preparing for the Mukilteo countdown
activities.description: Each lap needs to be faster than the last. In this case the “laps” were mile intervals, and I tried to go 15s faster each time. Needed a breather after 4.

activities.name: The bottle and the bottleneck
activities.description: With Expect Delays. Fun morning though I was completely unable to run up any part of the bigger hills. Oh well.

activities.name: Lots of stops

activities.name: Tangletown Calibration
activities.description: Better than yesterday, but a fair way from where I was before summer. Two stops

activities.description: Never simple!

activities.name: Dayton loop with a lot of walking
Sweet fall morning at the Disco
activities.description: With Lauren and Martha

activities.name: After the U-haul
activities.description: Drove a 20’ U haul from Ashland to here yesterday for Chris’ sister. Good I get out in the morning to the blue skies and scrubby hills of Montara

activities.name: Rattlesnake Ledge with the Snake2Lake crew
activities.description: They are on their way!

activities.name: Haul cart to and from first Aid Station
Meet Snake2Lake runners as they descend  Tiger
activities.description: To the second aid station. Both feeling pretty tired after 25+ miles and 5000 vertical feet

activities.name: Little dogs head run with the little dog
activities.description: Bean’s fastest run to date I think

activities.name: Simulating Gulch Countdown route
activities.description:
Event is on 10/19 in Mukilteo.  It’s going to be fun. Seattle people should sign up.

activities.name: Green Lake Man, with Martha, Wendy, and Anita

activities.name: Short Bean run after the rain

activities.description: On a crisp autumn morning

activities.name: Face time?
activities.description: Had planned to add a mullet and a pompadour to the face but that would have been another 4 miles. Too far for me at the moment.

activities.name: Benchmark Mountain from West Cady Ridge, with Ken, Mintu, and Herve
activities.description:
Most beautiful hike of the year. Glorious reds from the blueberry bushes, crisp air, views from Rainier to Stuart to Glacier Peak to Baker.

I classified it as a trail run because I ran the last two miles down.

activities.name: Impromptu Countdown with Herve and Anita
activities.description: The Japanese Gulch Countdown was postponed due to the wind, so we made a course of equivalent length and elevation, and did our own countdown. I survived 7 loops, starting super slow and ending at a decent clip.

activities.name: The new normal

activities.name: Yearly mileage goal achieved on the last day with the encouragement and help of my fam!
activities.description: An even 2000 km this year; my goal is to do less next year so I can do the strength training that I have been putting off for decades.
